channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How to file your taxes for free
item.description: Filing a tax return doesn't have to cost you money. While some may hire a tax preparation expert or purchase online software, millions of taxpayers can file state and federal returns for free. Here are some options to file your taxes for free before the April 15 deadline.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KTLA 5

item.title: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier, as the state’s attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat And Kate Payne, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge to rule swiftly on effort to block DOGE from assessing data and firing federal employees
item.description: A federal judge says she will quickly rule on a request to block Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing sensitive data and firing employees at several federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico awaits new response from Google on dispute over Gulf of Mexico name before filing lawsuit
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico said Monday it's awaiting a new response from Google to its request that the tech company fully restore the name Gulf of Mexico to its Google Maps service before filing a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Delta Airlines plane flips on landing in Toronto and a badly hurt child is among 19 injured
item.description: A badly injured child is among 19 people who were injured when a Delta Airlines plane flipped upon arrival at Toronto’s Pearson Airport.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, Fox 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The firings come just weeks after an American Airlines flight collided mid-air with a military Black Hawk helicopter shortly before landing at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican musical legend Paquita la del Barrio dies at 77
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican musical legend Paquita la del Barrio, known for her powerful voice and fierce defense of women, died at her home in Veracruz early Monday at the age of 77.
item.dc:creator: BERENICE BAUTISTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stolen sea monster carousel figurine recovered 40 years later
item.description: "We have gotten a priceless piece of our history back," Quassy President Eric Anderson said in a written statement.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, NewsNation

item.title: NASCAR Hall of Famer Jeff Gordon ready to partner with Tom Cruise on 'Days of Thunder' sequel
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Help him, Tom Cruise! NASCAR Hall of Famer Jeff Gordon wants a “Days of Thunder” sequel and said at Daytona International Speedway that he's been in touch with the Hollywood actor about making the project come to life.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US Forest Service layoffs raise concerns over Colorado wildfires, economy
item.description: “The USFS is already critically understaffed, and further employee cuts will have real and immediate consequences for Colorado’s economy, rural communities, and wildfire resilience.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Eagles player declines actor’s offer to pay Super Bowl fine
item.description: Actor and Eagles fan Rob McElhenny, best known for co-creating and starring in FXX’s “It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia,” had recently offered to foot the bill for a fine incurred by Eagles player Jalyx Hunt during Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illegal-smuggling coyotes now advertising at Canada border amid Trump migrant crackdown: report
item.description: Canada-based human smugglers are allegedly exploiting TikTok to advertise illegal entry into the US across the northern border amid Trump's southern crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: 3 dead in fiery Wyoming tunnel crash
item.description: Another person has been confirmed dead as the investigation continues into a major, multi-vehicle crash in the westbound tunnel of I-80 in Green River, Wyoming, on Friday, according to the Wyoming Dept. of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
